body:
Hey, got an email from Riverside Supply today. They're saying invoice #4821 for $1,340 is now 15 days overdue. Did you already handle this one or does it need to go through accounts payable?

Let me know so I can follow up with them if needed.
snippet: Hey, got an email from Riverside Supply today. They're saying invoice #4821 for ...
subject: Supplier invoice

body:
Thanks for the heads up. I submitted it to AP last week but I think it got stuck — I'll ping Sarah this afternoon and make sure it goes out by end of week.

I'll let Riverside know it's on the way. No worries.
snippet: Thanks for the heads up. I submitted it to AP last week but I think it got stuck...
subject: Re: Supplier invoice

body:
Hey Brian, quick heads up — Jake called in sick for Thursday's 6am shift and I don't have anyone to cover. Do you know if anyone on your end is available or should I reach out to the temp list?

Also, next week's schedule is still showing some gaps on Saturday afternoon. Can we sync before Wednesday?
snippet: Hey Brian, quick heads up — Jake called in sick for Thursday's 6am shift and I d...
subject: Staffing schedule

body:
Hey Tom, I can probably pull Marcus in for the Thursday morning slot — he mentioned he was open that week. Let me confirm with him and get back to you by tonight.

For Saturday, let's do a quick call Wednesday around noon if that works. I'll send a calendar invite.
snippet: Hey Tom, I can probably pull Marcus in for the Thursday morning slot — he mentio...
subject: Re: Staffing schedule

body:
Marcus would be perfect, thanks. Wednesday noon works great — accepted the invite.

Also just a heads up that the afternoon bootcamp class on Friday has 18 sign-ups now so we may want an extra trainer on standby. I'll add it to the agenda for Wednesday.
snippet: Marcus would be perfect, thanks. Wednesday noon works great — accepted the invit...
subject: Re: Staffing schedule

body:
Brian, just a reminder that Q2 estimated taxes are due June 15. Last year we almost missed it and got hit with a penalty. Did you already make the payment or do you want me to log into the IRS account and handle it?

Also, our accountant Linda wants us to send updated income numbers before the 10th so she can finalize the estimate.
snippet: Brian, just a reminder that Q2 estimated taxes are due June 15. Last year we alm...
subject: Quarterly tax estimate

body:
Good call — I hadn't done it yet. If you can pull the IRS login from LastPass and get Linda our YTD numbers, that would be a huge help. I think we're around $94k combined so far this year.

I can double-check the business side on my end and send you a breakdown by tomorrow morning.
snippet: Good call — I hadn't done it yet. If you can pull the IRS login from LastPass an...
subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate

body:
Got it. I'll email Linda the numbers today and ask her to run the estimate. Can you send me the Greenrider YTD by tonight? Even a rough figure works.

Also, I noticed our Q1 payment confirmation isn't in the shared folder — do you remember if we have a receipt for that? Linda might ask.
snippet: Got it. I'll email Linda the numbers today and ask her to run the estimate. Can ...
subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate

body:
I found the Q1 confirmation — emailing it to you now. The Greenrider YTD is approximately $58k gross; I'll send the exact number after I export the report.

Thanks for staying on top of this. I'll make the Q2 payment online once Linda confirms the amount.
snippet: I found the Q1 confirmation — emailing it to you now. The Greenrider YTD is appr...
subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate

body:
Brian, we got a pretty rough 2-star review on Google from a client named David M. He says he felt 'ignored' during his first session and that the trainer was on his phone. I think this was last Tuesday's intake session.

Do you want to draft a response or should I handle it? I want to make sure we address it professionally before it picks up traction.
snippet: Brian, we got a pretty rough 2-star review on Google from a client named David M...
subject: Customer review reply

body:
Ugh, not ideal. Let me look into which trainer was on Tuesday — if it's who I think it is, this isn't the first complaint. I'll talk to them directly.

For the reply, I'd keep it short and empathetic — acknowledge his experience, apologize, invite him to reach out directly. I can draft something and share it with you before we post. Give me an hour.
snippet: Ugh, not ideal. Let me look into which trainer was on Tuesday — if it's who I th...
subject: Re: Customer review reply

body:
Sounds good, I'll wait for your draft. And yeah, please look into Tuesday — I had a feeling something was off that day.

Also, while we're at it, we have a 5-star review from Sarah K. that's been sitting unacknowledged for two weeks. Can we reply to that one too? Good to show we engage with the positives.
snippet: Sounds good, I'll wait for your draft. And yeah, please look into Tuesday — I ha...
subject: Re: Customer review reply

body:
Draft for David M. is in the shared Google Doc — take a look and let me know if you want to adjust the tone. I tried to keep it warm but not over-apologetic.

Also added a quick reply to Sarah K.'s review in the same doc. Should take you 30 seconds to post. Let me know once both are live.
snippet: Draft for David M. is in the shared Google Doc — take a look and let me know if ...
subject: Re: Customer review reply

body:
Both look great — posted. The David M. response struck exactly the right note. I think we should set up a review monitoring routine so we're not letting these sit for weeks.

Also, Marcus told me it was Jason on his phone during Tuesday's session. I'll let you handle that conversation.
snippet: Both look great — posted. The David M. response struck exactly the right note. I...
subject: Re: Customer review reply

body:
Hey, any chance you're free for lunch this week? I know you've been swamped but it would be nice to actually sit down together for an hour. Thursday or Friday works best for me.

There's that new Thai place on Maple I've been wanting to try.
snippet: Hey, any chance you're free for lunch this week? I know you've been swamped but ...
subject: Lunch this week?

body:
Thursday works! I have a call until noon but should be free by 12:30. The Thai place sounds great — I'll make a reservation for 1pm just in case it's busy.

Looking forward to it. We're definitely overdue.
snippet: Thursday works! I have a call until noon but should be free by 12:30. The Thai p...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
Brian, I know this is a bit random but I feel like we never just talk anymore without the kids running around. Would you want to grab coffee Tuesday morning before work? There's a new place near the office that opened last month.

Nothing serious, just want to catch up properly!
snippet: Brian, I know this is a bit random but I feel like we never just talk anymore wi...
subject: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
Ha, I love that we're emailing each other to plan coffee dates. But yes, absolutely — Tuesday morning sounds great. What time? I can do 7:30 or 8:00 before my first meeting.

What's the name of the new place? I'll look it up.
snippet: Ha, I love that we're emailing each other to plan coffee dates. But yes, absolut...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
It's called Sunstone Coffee on 4th Ave — really good reviews. Let's do 7:45 so we have a little time before your 9am.

I'll put it on the family calendar so it doesn't get lost. See you there!
snippet: It's called Sunstone Coffee on 4th Ave — really good reviews. Let's do 7:45 so w...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
Hey! So Mom's birthday is the 14th and I was thinking we do a brunch at our place — nothing too big, just family and maybe the Hendersons. Probably 10 to 12 people.

Can you check if you have anything that Sunday morning? I want to start planning the menu and figure out if we're cooking or ordering from somewhere.
snippet: Hey! So Mom's birthday is the 14th and I was thinking we do a brunch at our plac...
subject: Birthday brunch plans

body:
The 14th works for me — nothing on my calendar. Brunch at home sounds perfect, Mom would love that over a restaurant.

Maybe we order from Corner Bakery for the pastry spread and do eggs ourselves? I can handle the mimosas. What does the Hendersons' RSVP situation look like?
snippet: The 14th works for me — nothing on my calendar. Brunch at home sounds perfect, M...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
I texted Linda Henderson and they're in. So we're looking at 11 people including us and the kids. Corner Bakery is a great idea — I'll place the order Thursday so we get the bulk discount.

Can you pick up OJ and champagne Saturday on your way home? And maybe some fresh fruit from the farmer's market if you have time.
snippet: I texted Linda Henderson and they're in. So we're looking at 11 people including...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
Will do — OJ, champagne, and fruit. I'll swing by the farmer's market Saturday morning with the kids so you can sleep in a little.

Should we get Mom flowers too or is that covered? I can grab a bouquet while I'm out.
snippet: Will do — OJ, champagne, and fruit. I'll swing by the farmer's market Saturday m...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
Brian, our home security monitoring service is up for renewal at the end of the month. The current plan is $44/month and they're offering an upgraded plan at $62/month that adds video monitoring and a cellular backup.

Is it worth upgrading or should we just renew as-is? I can call them if you want to negotiate the rate.
snippet: Brian, our home security monitoring service is up for renewal at the end of the ...
subject: Renewal options

body:
Honestly I think the cellular backup is worth it — remember when we lost WiFi during that storm in January and the alarm was basically useless? But $62 feels high. See if you can get them to $52 or so.

If they won't budge, SimpliSafe has similar coverage for under $30. Might be worth mentioning as leverage.
snippet: Honestly I think the cellular backup is worth it — remember when we lost WiFi du...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
Good point about the storm. I'll call them tomorrow and use SimpliSafe as the card to play — I looked them up and they're actually a real option if the negotiation doesn't go well.

Also saw in a forum that they have an unadvertised loyalty discount. I'll ask about that too.
snippet: Good point about the storm. I'll call them tomorrow and use SimpliSafe as the ca...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
Nice find on the loyalty discount. Let me know how the call goes. If they can land us around $50 with the upgrade, I'd say take it — probably not worth the hassle of switching for the savings.

Just make sure the new term isn't longer than 12 months. I don't want to be locked into two years.
snippet: Nice find on the loyalty discount. Let me know how the call goes. If they can la...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
I called — they offered the upgraded plan at $51/month with the loyalty discount on a 12-month contract. I said yes.

New plan starts the 1st and they're throwing in a free door sensor. I think we did pretty well!
snippet: I called — they offered the upgraded plan at $51/month with the loyalty discount...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
Hey, I was going through the business account and noticed an invoice from Hartwell Printing for $870 that I don't recognize. It's dated May 22nd and the description just says 'branded materials.'

Do you know what this is for? I want to make sure it's legit before I mark it as approved.
snippet: Hey, I was going through the business account and noticed an invoice from Hartwe...
subject: Vendor invoice question

body:
Yes, that's the batch of folders and branded notebooks we ordered for the June sales conference. Totally legit — go ahead and approve it.

Sorry, I should have flagged that when the order went in. I'll give you a heads up on vendor orders going forward so nothing looks unfamiliar.
snippet: Yes, that's the batch of folders and branded notebooks we ordered for the June s...
subject: Re: Vendor invoice question

body:
Brian, I heard back from the property manager about the lease renewal for Aunt Diane's unit. They're offering a 12-month renewal at $1,580/month (up from $1,490) or a 6-month at $1,640/month.

Diane is okay with whatever we decide but wanted our input. What do you think makes more sense?
snippet: Brian, I heard back from the property manager about the lease renewal for Aunt D...
subject: Apartment lease renewal

body:
The 12-month makes more sense financially — $90/month less and it gives Diane stability. The only reason I'd consider 6 months is if she might want to move before next year, but I don't think that's likely.

Did the property manager say anything about parking or utilities changing in the new rate?
snippet: The 12-month makes more sense financially — $90/month less and it gives Diane st...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body:
Parking is still included same as before, utilities are separate — no changes there.

Diane confirmed she's not planning to move so I'll tell them 12-month and get the paperwork sent to her directly. Thanks for weighing in!
snippet: Parking is still included same as before, utilities are separate — no changes th...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body:
Forwarding this week's digest from that Saturday Morning Brew newsletter I told you about. There's a really interesting piece on remote work culture that made me think of the stuff you've been dealing with at Greenrider.

Also a quick read on home energy savings — some of those tips actually look practical for us.
snippet: Forwarding this week's digest from that Saturday Morning Brew newsletter I told ...
subject: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Ha, how many newsletters are you subscribed to now? I'll check out the remote work piece tonight — the timing is good since we're in the middle of revisiting our hybrid policy.

Send me the energy one too if it's a separate link. We've had surprisingly high electric bills this spring.
snippet: Ha, how many newsletters are you subscribed to now? I'll check out the remote wo...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Only four, I promise. The energy article is in the same issue, second section — I'll paste the direct link so it's easier to find.

Also the newsletter has a weekend events section and there's a pop-up food market downtown next Saturday that looks really fun if the weather holds.
snippet: Only four, I promise. The energy article is in the same issue, second section — ...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
The remote work article was genuinely good — I'm going to share it with our ops team. Bookmarked the energy tips too, a few of those were legitimately useful.

The food market sounds great. Let's plan for it assuming the forecast isn't terrible. Can you add it to the calendar?
snippet: The remote work article was genuinely good — I'm going to share it with our ops ...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Hey, just want to confirm pickups for next week. I have my dentist appointment Tuesday at 3:30 and a work call Thursday that usually runs until 4. Can you cover both of those days or do we need to ask Mom to help out?

Emma's practice runs until 4:15 on Tuesday and Jake's school ends at 3:45.
snippet: Hey, just want to confirm pickups for next week. I have my dentist appointment T...
subject: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
I can do Jake's pickup Tuesday no problem. For Emma's 4:15 I might be cutting it close with a 3pm call — can she wait with Coach Williams until 4:30 if needed? Otherwise I'll ask your mom to grab her.

Thursday I can do both — I'll leave the office by 3:30.
snippet: I can do Jake's pickup Tuesday no problem. For Emma's 4:15 I might be cutting it...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Coach Williams is totally fine with that, I asked last time and she said it's never an issue. So Tuesday: you get Jake, and grab Emma by 4:30 if your call runs over.

Thursday works perfectly. I'll update the shared calendar. Mom will be relieved she doesn't have to drive over.
snippet: Coach Williams is totally fine with that, I asked last time and she said it's ne...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Perfect. I'll block 3:30 on my calendar both days as a hard stop. If anything changes on my end I'll text you right away.

Also — is Jake's soccer bag still in the trunk of your car? I couldn't find it this morning and he'll need it Tuesday.
snippet: Perfect. I'll block 3:30 on my calendar both days as a hard stop. If anything ch...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Yes, it's in my trunk — I'll leave it in the front hall tonight so you have it. His cleats are in there too.

Thanks for sorting all this out. Next week's going to be hectic but I think we've got it covered.
snippet: Yes, it's in my trunk — I'll leave it in the front hall tonight so you have it. ...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Brian, I started looking into options for the summer trip and narrowed it down to two: a beach rental in the Outer Banks the week of July 21st, or a cabin in the Smokies the week of August 4th. Prices are similar — the beach house is a bit more at $2,800 for the week versus $2,100 for the cabin.

The kids seem excited about both. Do you have a preference or any dates that don't work?
snippet: Brian, I started looking into options for the summer trip and narrowed it down t...
subject: Family vacation planning

body:
I'm leaning toward the Outer Banks — the kids haven't seen the ocean in two years and I think they'd love it. August 4th doesn't work anyway, we have my cousin's wedding that weekend.

If the July 21st house is still available, let's move on it before it fills up. Can you send me the listing? I'd like to see the layout before we commit.
snippet: I'm leaning toward the Outer Banks — the kids haven't seen the ocean in two year...
subject: Re: Family vacation planning

body:
Hey, what are we doing this weekend? I don't think we have anything locked in. Emma mentioned she wants to go to the splash pad and Jake's been asking about the science museum for weeks.

I could also just use a low-key Saturday at home if you're exhausted from the week. What sounds good to you?
snippet: Hey, what are we doing this weekend? I don't think we have anything locked in. E...
subject: Weekend plans

body:
Let's do the science museum Saturday and the splash pad Sunday — that way both kids get their pick. I'll check the museum hours; I think they open at 10.

Saturday evening maybe we order pizza and watch something after the kids are in bed? I feel like we haven't had a proper movie night in a while.
snippet: Let's do the science museum Saturday and the splash pad Sunday — that way both k...
subject: Re: Weekend plans

body:
That sounds like the perfect weekend honestly — I'm in for all of it, especially the movie night. I'll let both kids know their activity is on the schedule so they stop lobbying me.

Any movie ideas? I've heard good things about that new thriller on Netflix but I'm also happy with a rewatch if you have something in mind.
snippet: That sounds like the perfect weekend honestly — I'm in for all of it, especially...
subject: Re: Weekend plans

body:
Brian,

Dad and I are looking at flights over Labor Day. If y'all can host that weekend I'll bring the potato salad and the pecan pie. Let me know how the twins' soccer schedule looks.

Love,
Mom
